name: webpage_links_extractor
title: Webpage Links Extractor
description: Retrieve a rendered page and normalize internal or external links with anchor text, relationship labels, and domains.

seo.title: Webpage Links Extractor Tool for Structured Web Data
seo.description: Retrieve a rendered page and normalize internal or external links with anchor text, relationship labels, and domains. Better Fetch returns clean structured records ready for agents, exports, monitoring, and downstream workflows.
seo.intro: Retrieve a rendered page and normalize internal or external links with anchor text, relationship labels, and domains. This first-party Better Fetch tool turns a focused request into predictable structured output that can be inspected in the web workspace, exported as JSON or CSV, called through REST, or composed by an AI agent over MCP. It is designed for repeatable production research rather than fragile copy-and-paste scraping.

useCases.title: Agent research
useCases.description: Give AI agents bounded, normalized webpage links extractor results without writing source-specific retrieval and parsing code.

useCases.title: Repeatable monitoring
useCases.description: Save the configuration, rerun it on a consistent source, and export comparable structured artifacts for operational review.

faqs.question: What does Webpage Links Extractor return?
faqs.answer: It returns normalized structured fields documented in the output schema, plus the fetched source URL and a record count for receipts and exports.

faqs.question: Does this tool store the retrieved output?
faqs.answer: No. Better Fetch records privacy-safe run metadata for reliability and retention analysis, but the output payload itself is not stored by the workspace.

title.description: Resolved page or Open Graph title

description.description: Open Graph or standard page description

image.description: Open Graph image URL when exposed
